  Looks up an existing Barndoor access policy by id or by name. Use it to reference a policy that is not managed by this Terraform configuration, or as the lookup step before a terraform import. # barndoor_policy (Data Source)

Looks up an existing Barndoor access policy by `id` or by `name`. Use it to reference a policy that is not managed by this Terraform configuration, or as the lookup step before a `terraform import`. Archived policies are not returned: archival is the platform's terminal lifecycle state, equivalent to deletion.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing access policy by name (or by id) to reference it from
# other configuration without managing it — for example to mirror its agent
# bindings onto a new policy, or to find the id for a `terraform import`.
data "barndoor_policy" "sales_guardrails" {
  name = "Sales guardrails"
}

output "sales_guardrails_status" {
  value = data.barndoor_policy.sales_guardrails.status
}

# The looked-up policy's agent bindings can seed other resources.
resource "barndoor_policy" "sales_guardrails_v2" {
  name            = "Sales guardrails v2"
  mcp_server_id   = data.barndoor_policy.sales_guardrails.mcp_server_id
  application_ids = data.barndoor_policy.sales_guardrails.application_ids

  rules = [{
    effect  = "ALLOW"
    actions = ["*"]
    roles   = ["group:sales"]
  }]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `id` (String) Policy UUID. Exactly one of `id` or `name` must be set.
- `name` (String) Policy name. Exactly one of `id` or `name` must be set. Names are unique among the organization's non-archived policies; the comparison is exact.

### Read-Only

- `application_ids` (Set of String) IDs of the AI Agents (applications) this policy applies to.
- `description` (String) Free-form description of the policy.
- `mcp_server_id` (String) ID of the MCP server the policy governs.
- `rules` (Attributes List) Ordered rules evaluated against tool calls. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status: `DRAFT`, `ACTIVE`, or `INACTIVE`.
- `support_contact` (String) Contact shown to end users when this policy blocks them.
- `tags` (Set of String) Organizational tags attached to the policy.

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Read-Only:

- `actions` (List of String) Tool/action names the rule matches (`*` matches all).
- `active` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enforced.
- `condition` (String) Condition tree as canonical JSON, when the rule carries one.
- `description` (String) Free-form description of the rule.
- `effect` (String) Whether matching calls are allowed or denied: `ALLOW` or `DENY`.
- `name` (String) Rule name.
- `roles` (List of String) Principals the rule matches: `role:<name>`, `group:<name>`, or `*`.
